steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The graph passes through $(-5,0)$, $(1,0)$, and $(4,0)$, so the zeros of $f$ are $x = -5$, $x = 1$, and $x = 4$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: By the Factor Theorem: if $f(a) = 0$, then $(x - a)$ is a factor. So the factors corresponding to the zeros are $(x+5)$, $(x-1)$, and $(x-4)$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Scan the choices for any of these three factors: $(x+5)$ is not listed; $(x-1)$ is the correct answer; $(x-4)$ is not listed.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content: The matching choice, $(x-1)$, must be a factor because $f(1) = 0$.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $x+1$: This factor implies a zero at $x = -1$. The graph does not pass through $(-1, 0)$, so $(x+1)$ need not be a factor. ✗
  • Choice B — $x+4$: This factor implies a zero at $x = -4$. The graph does not pass through $(-4, 0)$, so $(x+4)$ need not be a factor. ✗
  • Choice C — $x-1$: This factor implies a zero at $x = 1$. The graph passes through $(1, 0)$, confirming $f(1) = 0$, so $(x-1)$ must be a factor by the Factor Theorem. ✓
  • Choice D — $x-5$: This factor implies a zero at $x = 5$. The graph does not pass through $(5, 0)$, so $(x-5)$ need not be a factor. ✗
C is correct.
